title: Phytochemical screening and flavonoid quantification of Cymbopogon citratus (inflorescence)
author: Rout, Subhalakshmi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Cymbopogon citratus (lemon grass) is an economically and medicinally important aromatic grass widely used in traditional medicine and nutraceutical formulations. Present study aimed to evaluate the qualitative and quantitative phytochemical composition of C. citratus inflorescence using different solvent extracts. Primary and secondary metabolites were screened through standard phytochemical assays, while total flavonoid content was quantified using the aluminium chloride colorimetric method with quercetin as the standard. The qualitative analysis revealed that non-polar extracts showed no detectable metabolites, whereas polar extracts exhibited a broad spectrum of bioactive compounds. Aqueous extracts showed the richest profile, containing proteins, carbohydrates, starch, tannins, flavonoids and reducing sugars, indicating that most metabolites in C. citratus are water-soluble. Quantitative estimation revealed a total flavonoid content of 31.31 mg/100 g, suggesting moderate antioxidant potential of the inflorescence. The current work highlights the phytochemical richness and potential therapeutic value of C. citratus, especially in its polar extracts.
